result.status_detail: APF envelope delivered (200, 16/15/8 structure with warning text) but Mistral UI showed Error 6002 fallback
console_errors: 1 console error (likely client-side parse exception triggering Error 6002 fallback)
result.verdict_classification: PARTIAL -- APF envelope returned per Network panel with correct 16/15/8 structure and warning text containing keywords (⚠️/민감/보안), but the Mistral client-side parser rejected the message and rendered an 'Error code: 6002' fallback page in place of the assistant chat bubble.
result.rendered_text_in_chat_area:
Something went wrong
It appears that an unexpected issue has occurred. We apologize for the inconvenience. Please try again later or refresh the page. If the problem persists, feel free to contact our support team for assistance.
Error code: 6002
result.stage1_note: message.newChat (Stage 1 tRPC) was NOT triggered for this turn because chat session already existed from prior test (continuation chat). Only /api/chat (Stage 2) fired this turn -- which is the intended Stage-2-only intercept target.

screenshots_inventory.description: Chat UI showing 'Something went wrong / Error code: 6002' red error fallback in place of assistant message bubble; user PII prompt visible above
Network Response tab showing the 3-line NDJSON envelope (16:disclaimers, 15:message, 8:null) returned by /api/chat
Network Response tab full view after clicking Response sub-tab; raw envelope text visible
Verbatim text dump of /api/chat response body (3 lines: 16:disclaimers, 15:message with warning text, 8:null) -- machine-readable copy for dev team analysis

result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Envelope delivery layer WORKS (status 200, content-type text/plain, 3-line structure correct, warning text visible in body). Failure is at Mistral CLIENT PARSER. Mistral renders 'Error 6002' fallback instead of parsing the {'type':'message','message':{...}} chunk into a chat bubble. Likely root cause: the assistant message object is missing fields the Mistral client requires for an assistant turn -- candidates per #594 capture: createdAt (ISO timestamp), status (e.g. 'COMPLETED'), modelId, conversationId, parentMessageId, version. Recommendation: capture a real successful Mistral chat response from the same chat (continuation turn, NOT first turn) and DIFF its '15:{json:...}' message object against ours. Add the missing fields to the envelope template. Also check whether the Mistral client expects messages to be wrapped in a different chunk type for stream-injected (vs newChat-originated) responses. Note: Stage-2-only strategy was the right choice -- Stage 1 untouched + envelope delivered without aborting connection (improvement over you.com 595 which failed at delivery layer).
result.previous_classification: Pre-596: mistral_ndjson_v2 envelope was applied (id=24, 429B) but never tested end-to-end. This is the FIRST test of the new template built from #594 captured schema (Stage-2-only intercept).
result.diff_from_previous_understanding: Stage-2-only intercept design is correct (intercept fires on /api/chat only, Stage 1 untouched). Envelope SHAPE (16:disclaimers + 15:message + 8:null) is correct per Vercel AI SDK protocol. BUT message object's internal schema is incomplete -- missing fields needed by Mistral client to render an assistant turn. Failure mode is at parser, NOT delivery. Different from you.com 595 where delivery itself failed (RST/0B body); here delivery succeeds and bytes-on-wire are visible -- this is a content/schema gap, not infrastructure.
obstacles_encountered: Chat already had a previous turn (Capital of South Korea) so Stage 1 (message.newChat) did not fire on this turn -- could not directly verify Stage 1 untouched, but absence in network log confirms only /api/chat was hit

Mistral UI renders 'Error 6002' rather than the warning text; envelope shape (15:message with role=assistant + content=[{type:text,text:...}]) appears not to match what the Mistral client parser expects for an assistant turn -- likely missing required fields (e.g. createdAt, status, or wrapping in a different chunk type), causing the page to throw and fall back to error UI

notes: Cycle 94 R-16 mistral_ndjson_v2 envelope verification: PARTIAL. Different failure mode from #595. APF envelope DELIVERED correctly (200 status, full 16/15/8 structure with warning text containing ⚠️/민감/보안). BUT Mistral client parser rejected the message object → 'Error code: 6002' fallback rendered. Envelope BODY captured verbatim (see chat_response_body.txt). Likely root cause: missing required fields on the assistant message object (candidates: createdAt, status, modelId, conversationId, parentMessageId, version per #594 capture). Recommend: diff our '15:{message}' against a real successful Mistral chat response (continuation turn) and add missing fields. Stage-2-only strategy worked at delivery layer (vs #595 you.com which failed at delivery). 4 artifacts in files/596/ (3 PNGs + 1 txt response body dump). Processed via 19th governance Subagent Dispatch pattern (3m24s, 40 tool uses).
